MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-15-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Florence A. McClain, Esq. of WALSWORTH FRANKLIN BEVINS & McCALL (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant / moving party SB Decking Inc.
Gibert L. Purcell, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Kenneth Johnson

RULING:
Defendant SB Decking Inc.'s motion for summary judgment. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion is denied. Defendant has not satisfied its burden of production on the evidence-free prong due to the absence of any responses to "all facts" interrogatories regarding plaintiff's exposure to asbestos-containing products attributable to defendant. Mr. Spaeth's declaration does not negate the ability of plaintiff to prove exposure. Even if defendant had satisfied its burden, plaintiff has created a triable issue regarding exposure by Marbley's declaration and defendant's interrogatory responses. Defendant's objections to Marbley's declaration are overruled. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Maria Lertora Aliventi, CSR # 11935

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-15-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Michelle M. Clowser, Esq. of BRYDON HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant / moving party NATIONAL STEEL AND SHIPBUILDING COMPANY
Gibert L. Purcell, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Kenneth Johnson

RULING:
Defendant NATIONAL STEEL AND SHIPBUILDING COMPANY's motion for summary judgment. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion is denied. Defendant failed to satisfy its burden of production on the issue of whether Johnson was directly exposed to asbestos-containing components on ships built by National Steel. Defendant's separate statement and citations to evidence therein focuses only on a re-entrainment theory of exposure and do not show that plaintiff lacks evidence to prove his direct exposure to an original component part of a ship manufactured by defendant. Defendant's motion for summary adjudications as to false representation and punitive damages is moot, causes of action dismissed previously. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Maria Lertora Aliventi, CSR # 11935
